description: If a taxpayer discovers an omission or wrong statement in a filed return, they can file a revised return u/s 139(5). The revised return replaces the original. Can be filed multiple times before the deadline.
timeline: Deadline: March 31, 2027 (3 years from end of AY 2026-27, capped at before completion of assessment, whichever is earlier)
eligibility: Original return must be filed (cannot revise if original was not filed)
Revision must be before completion of assessment u/s 143(3)
Revised return replaces original — original stands withdrawn
Cannot reduce income already assessed by AO — only errors/omissions
commonRevisionsReason: Missed income head (forgot to include rental income)

steps.description: Review original ITR, Form 26AS, AIS carefully. Note specific changes needed.
Log into e-filing portal. Select 'File Income Tax Return'. Select AY 2026-27. Select 'Revised Return u/s 139(5)'. Enter original acknowledgment number and date.
Fill in corrected details. Compute revised tax liability. Pay additional tax if any via self-assessment (Challan 280). Submit and e-verify.

commonMistakes: Filing revised return after March 31, 2027 (not accepted — belated return window closes)
Not paying interest u/s 234A/234B on additional tax before filing revision
Trying to reduce income in revised return that was already assessed — AO can reopen original assessment
consequences: If tax due not paid before revision: Interest u/s 234B continues to accrue
Cannot revise after assessment u/s 143(3) is completed — use rectification u/s 154 instead
